LAW AND MOTION 302, DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. NO OTHER APPEARANCE. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANTS ROBERT DREYER, ROBERT DREYER INC., ACCOUNTANT, CRISTINA DREYER INC., ACCOUNTANT, DAVID LUCHETTI, DAVID LUCHETTI INC., ACCOUNTANT, DREYER, GEYER, LUCHETTI INC., ACCOUNTANT, AND CHRISTINA DREYER'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED AS TO THE TENTH CAUSE OF ACTION, SUSTAINED WITH TWENTY DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE FIFTH AND SEVENTH CAUSES OF ACTION AND SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE NINTH AND ELEVENTH CAUSE OF ACTION. THE CIVIL EMBEZZLEMENT CLAIM IS ADEQUATELY PLED. THE 17200 CLAIM DOES NOT ADEQUATELY ALLEGED ANY UNLAWFUL, UNFAIR OR FRAUDULENT BUSINESS PRACTICE, AND PLAINTIFFS ARE GIVEN LEAVE TO CURE THIS DEFICIENCY IF THEY CAN DO SO IN GOOD FAITH. THE FRAUD CLAIM DOES NOT PLEAD ALL OF THE ELEMENTS OF ACTIONABLE FRAUD WITH THE REQUIRED PARTICULARITY, AND PLAINTIFFS ARE GIVEN LEAVE TO CURE THIS DEFICIENCY IF THEY CAN DO SO IN GOOD FAITH. THE CONVERSION CLAIM IS DEFICIENT BECAUSE IT DOES NOT ALLEGE THE RIGHT TO IMMEDIATELY POSSESS A SPECIFIC SUM OF MONEY, AND IT DOES NOT APPEAR THAT PLAINTIFFS CAN CURE THIS DEFICIENCY. THE INTENTIONAL INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS CLAIM IS DEFICIENT BECAUSE IT DOES NOT ALLEGE EXTREME AND OUTRAGEOUS CONDUCT, AND IT DOES NOT APPEAR THAT PLAINTIFFS CAN CURE THIS DEFICIENCY. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN; COURT REPORTER: LORETTA NAJERA CSR # 5153. (302)

LAW AND MOTION 302, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANTS ROBERT DREYER; ROBERT DREYER INC., ACCOUNTANT; CHRISTINA DREYER INC., ACCOUNTANT; DAVID LUCHETTI; DAVID LUCHETTI INC., ACCOUNTANT; DREYER, GEYER, LUCHETTI INC., ACCOUNTANT; CHRISTINA DREYER'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH TEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. AS TO THE FIFTH CAUSE OF ACTION, THE 17200 CLAIM DOES NOT ADEQUATELY ALLEGE ANY UNLAWFUL OR FRAUDULENT BUSINESS PRACTICE. PLAINTIFF HAS FAILED TO ALLEGE ANY SPECIFIC LAW 'BORROWED' FOR THE "UNLAWFUL" PRONG OF 17200. TO ASSERT THE "FRAUDULENT BUSINESS PRACTICE" PRONG, PLAINTIFF MUST SHOW ACTUAL RELIANCE ON THE SPECIFIED ALLEGED MISREPRESENTATION, AS WELL AS THE SPECIFICITY REFERRED TO BELOW. PLAINTIFF HAS FAILED TO ALLEGE RELIANCE. PLAINTIFF IS GRANTED LEAVE TO AMEND, IF HE CAN DO SO IN GOOD FAITH, TO CURE THE DEFECTS. AS TO THE SEVENTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR FRAUD, THE ELEMENTS THAT MUST BE PLEADED WITH PARTICULARITY INCLUDE: (1) MISREPRESENTATION; (2) KNOWLEDGE OF FALSITY; (3) INTENT TO DEFRAUD; (4) JUSTIFIABLE RELIANCE; AND (5) RESULTING DAMAGE. PHILIPSON & SIMON V. GULSVIG (2007) 154 CA4TH 347, 363. PLAINTIFF HAS FAILED TO PLEAD FACTS WITH SUFFICIENT PARTICULARITY AS TO EACH ELEMENT FOR THE FRAUD CLAIM. PLAINTIFF IS GRANTED LEAVE TO AMEND. THE COURT DOES NOT CONTEMPLATE THAT FURTHER LEAVE TO AMEND WILL BE GRANTED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: CURTIS E.A. KARNOW; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; REPORTER: MELANIE GHENO CSR # 7489

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-27-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Maria A. Yuen, Esq. of Stein Law Group, Tel. 415.321.7190, appearing for Defendant and moving party.

No other appearance. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTs ROBERT DREYER; ROBERT DREYER INC., ACCOUNTANT; CHRISTINA DREYER INC., ACCOUNTANT; DAVID LUCHETTI; DAVID LUCHETTI INC., ACCOUNTANT; DREYER, GEYER, LUCHETTI INC., ACCOUNTANT; CHRISTINA DREYER's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is Sustained with ten days leave to amend. As to the fifth cause of action, the 17200 claim does not adequately allege any unlawful or fraudulent business practice. Plaintiff has failed to allege any specific law 'borrowed' for the "unlawful" prong of 17200. To assert the "fraudulent business practice" prong, Plaintiff must show actual reliance on the specified alleged misrepresentation, as well as the specificity referred to below. Plaintiff has failed to allege reliance. Plaintiff is granted leave to amend, if he can do so in good faith, to cure the defects. As to the seventh cause of action for fraud, the elements that must be pleaded with particularity include: (1) misrepresentation; (2) knowledge of falsity; (3) intent to defraud; (4) justifiable reliance; and (5) resulting damage. Philipson & Simon v. Gulsvig (2007) 154 CA4th 347, 363. Plaintiff has failed to plead facts with sufficient particularity as to each element for the fraud claim. Plaintiff is granted leave to amend. The Court does not contemplate that further leave to amend will be granted. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Curtis E.A. Karnow; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Melanie Gheno CSR # 7489

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-08-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Maria A. Yuen, Esq., of Stein Law Group, Tel. 415.321.7190, appearing for defendants and moving party.

No other Appearance. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS ROBERT DREYER, ROBERT DREYER INC., ACCOUNTANT, CRISTINA DREYER INC., ACCOUNTANT, DAVID LUCHETTI, DAVID LUCHETTI INC., ACCOUNTANT, DREYER, GEYER, LUCHETTI INC., ACCOUNTANT, and CHRISTINA DREYER'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is Overruled as to the tenth cause of action, sustained with twenty days leave to amend as to the fifth and seventh causes of action and sustained without leave to amend as to the ninth and eleventh cause of action. The civil embezzlement claim is adequately pled. The 17200 claim does not adequately alleged any unlawful, unfair or fraudulent business practice, and plaintiffs are given leave to cure this deficiency if they can do so in good faith. The fraud claim does not plead all of the elements of actionable fraud with the required particularity, and plaintiffs are given leave to cure this deficiency if they can do so in good faith. The conversion claim is deficient because it does not allege the right to immediately possess a specific sum of money, and it does not appear that plaintiffs can cure this deficiency. The intentional infliction of emotional distress claim is deficient because it does not allege extreme and outrageous conduct, and it does not appear that plaintiffs can cure this deficiency. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Court Reporter: Loretta Najera CSR # 5153
